source_pr.title: infoschema: fix unstable test TestSelectClusterTable
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: crazycs520 <crazycs520@gmail.com>

Issue Number: close #41307 

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: TestSelectClusterTable is unstable because it relies on a slow-log file path configured via global config; using the default slow log file can conflict with other tests or not point to the prepared file.
failure_signature: TestSelectClusterTable: slow log/cluster table assertions fail because the server is not using the prepared slow-log file (global SlowQueryFile mismatch)
root_cause_explanation: The flakiness was caused by shared global configuration/state: the test prepared a slow log file but did not ensure TiDB was configured to write/read slow queries from that file (and the default slow-log filename can be shared across tests). The fix uses a unique slow log filename and updates the global config (Log.SlowQueryFile) to point to it, ensuring the cluster table query reads the intended slow log data deterministically.
fix_pattern: Use a unique slow-log filename and update global config to point SlowQueryFile at it to avoid cross-test interference.

evidence: evidence: infoschema: fix unstable test TestSelectClusterTable
